name: Google Calendar
description: Read and write Google Calendar — list subscribed calendars (including shared family calendars), fetch events, create/update/delete events. 6 tools, OAuth2 user auth.
instructions:
This connector uses the Google Calendar API v3 (developers.google.com/calendar).

**Shared calendars (e.g. your wife's):** OAuth runs as **you**. Any calendar visible in your Google Calendar sidebar — including calendars shared with you — appears in `gcal_list_calendars`. You can **read** any calendar you can see; **writes** require `accessRole` of `writer` or `owner` on that calendar (you can create events on your primary; on her calendar only if she granted edit access).

**Setup — Google Cloud (one-time)**:
1. https://console.cloud.google.com → create or pick a project.
2. **APIs & Services → Library** → enable **Google Calendar API**.
3. **OAuth consent screen** → External (or Internal if Workspace) → add scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar` → add yourself as a **Test user** while the app is in Testing.
4. **Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID** → type **Web application**.
5. **Authorized redirect URI** (exact):
   `https://personal-os-mcp.onrender.com/api/mcp-oauth/callback`
6. Copy **Client ID** and **Client secret** into the import form as `GOOGLE_CALENDAR_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_CALENDAR_CLIENT_SECRET`.
7. Import adapter → **Authorize with Provider** → sign in with your Google account.

**Scope:** `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar` — full read/write on calendars you can access in the Google Calendar UI.

**Typical read flow:**
1. `gcal_list_calendars` — find `id`, `summary`, `accessRole`, `primary`.
2. `gcal_list_events` — events for `primary` or a shared calendar id.

**Typical write flow:**
1. Confirm `accessRole` is `writer` or `owner` on the target calendar.
2. `gcal_create_event` — summary + start/end (timed or all-day).
3. `gcal_update_event` / `gcal_delete_event` — need `eventId` from list/get.

**calendarId:** use `primary` or the `id` from `gcal_list_calendars` (often an email for shared calendars).

**Re-auth after scope change:** if you previously authorized with readonly scope, delete the connector OAuth tokens (re-authorize) so Google issues a token with the new scope.

tools.name: gcal_list_calendars
tools.description: List all calendars on the authenticated user's calendar list — primary calendar, your secondary calendars, and calendars shared with you (family, work, etc.). Returns id, summary, description, timeZone, accessRole, primary, selected.

pageToken.description: Pagination token from a previous response.

minAccessRole.description: Filter: freeBusyReader, reader, writer, or owner.

tools.name: gcal_list_events
tools.description: List events on one calendar. Use calendarId=primary for your main calendar, or an id from gcal_list_calendars (including a shared family calendar).

singleEvents.description: Expand recurring events into instances (default true).

orderBy.description: startTime or updated (requires singleEvents=true for startTime).

q.description: Free-text search on title, description, location, attendees.

tools.name: gcal_get_event
tools.description: Fetch a single event by calendarId + eventId.

tools.name: gcal_create_event
tools.description: Create an event on a calendar. Requires writer or owner accessRole on that calendar. For timed events pass startDateTime + endDateTime (+ optional timeZone). For all-day events pass startDate + endDate (YYYY-MM-DD, end exclusive).

timeZone.description: IANA timezone for timed events, e.g. America/New_York.

sendUpdates.description: all, externalOnly, or none — whether to email attendees.

tools.name: gcal_update_event
tools.description: Patch an existing event. Only include fields to change. Same start/end rules as gcal_create_event.

tools.name: gcal_delete_event
tools.description: Delete an event from a calendar. Requires writer or owner access on the calendar.
